summary:
The agreement grants ADAMS GOLF the exclusive right to use professional golfer Tom Watson's name, likeness, and endorsement for promoting its golf products. Watson must exclusively use specified ADAMS GOLF products during professional events and cannot endorse competing products. ADAMS GOLF is obligated to provide Watson with necessary products and pay a base compensation, with potential performance bonuses based on his achievements.

Notable risks include Watson's obligation to repay a portion of his compensation if he fails to meet a minimum number of tournament appearances. Additionally, either party can terminate the agreement for material breaches, with Watson required to repay for unfulfilled contract periods. ADAMS GOLF may also terminate if Watson's conduct diminishes the value of his endorsement. Both parties indemnify each other against liabilities arising from breaches of the agreement.
termination_clause: if either party commits any material breach of this Agreement, the other party may terminate for cause upon giving fifteen (15) days written notice of such cause and provided the breach is not rectified within such fifteen (15) day period.
confidentiality_clause: both parties agree not to disclose the terms of this Agreement, without the permission of the other party, to any third party other than to CONSULTANT'S business, legal and financial advisors, and with respect to all such advisors, CONSULTANT shall take all reasonable steps to ensure such confidentiality to ADAMS GOLF.
liability_clause: CONSULTANT agrees to protect, indemnify and hold ADAMS GOLF harmless from any and all liability, claims, causes of action, suits, damages and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees and expenses) for which it becomes liable or is compelled to pay by reason of a breach of any covenant or representation by CONSULTANT in this Agreement.

summary: The Joint Content License Agreement establishes a cooperative marketing and licensing relationship between WPT Enterprises, Inc. and Zynga Inc. to promote each other's brands through various gaming platforms. Zynga is obligated to pay WPT an annual minimum guarantee of $3 million and a 10% royalty on net revenue from WPT-branded content. WPT must promote Zynga's brand at its tournaments, while Zynga will create a WPT-branded tournament mode in its games. Notable risks include potential breaches of contract, which could lead to termination and liability for damages. Each party indemnifies the other against third-party claims arising from breaches of their respective obligations. The agreement also limits liability for indirect damages and specifies that both parties must maintain adequate insurance coverage.
termination_clause: Each party shall have the right at any time to terminate this Agreement without prejudice to any rights which it may have, whether pursuant to the provisions of this Agreement or otherwise in law or in equity or otherwise, upon the occurrence of any one or more of the following events: i. The other party breaches or fails to perform any of its material obligations provided for in this Agreement; ii. The other party is unable to pay its debts when due, or makes any assignment for the benefit of creditors, or files any petition under the bankruptcy or insolvency laws of any jurisdiction, county or place, or has or suffers a receiver or trustee to be appointed for its business or property, or is adjudicated a bankrupt or an insolvent; or iii. The other party asserts any rights in or to the terminating party's intellectual property in violation of this Agreement.
confidentiality_clause: The parties acknowledge and agree that the subject matter of this Agreement constitutes "Business Purpose" and this Agreement and any Exhibits hereunder are "Confidential Information" of the parties as defined as "Information" in the Non-Disclosure Agreement between the parties dated August 24, 2017, and accordingly the restrictions relating to confidentiality and use thereof provided in the Non-Disclosure Agreement apply to any party's Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement.
liability_clause: EXCEPT IN CASES OF GROSS NEGLIGENCE, WILLFUL MISCONDUCT OR FRAUD, INDEMNIFICATION CLAIMS UNDER SECTION 5 OR BREACHES OF SECTION 2 (TRADEMARKS), 8 (CONFIDENTIALITY), OR 9 (NO AGENCY RELATIONSHIP), IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY OR ITS OFFICERS, DIRECTORS, OR EMPLOYEES BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER PARTY IN CONNECTION WITH THE SUBJECT MATTER HEREOF, FOR ANY SPECIAL, INDIRECT, CONSEQUENTIAL OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES OF ANY KIND, LOST PROFITS OR LOST REVENUE, WHETHER ARISING IN CONTRACT, TORT, NEGLIGENCE, STATUTE, OR OTHERWISE, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY THEREOF.

summary: The Licensing and Website Hosting Agreement establishes a partnership between Mortgage Logic.com, Inc. (Client) and TrueLink, Inc. (TrueLink) for access to TrueLink's interface software and related support services. TrueLink is obligated to provide the Client with a non-exclusive license to use the interface, hosting services, and technical support, while the Client must comply with applicable laws and maintain confidentiality regarding proprietary information. Notable risks include potential liability for breaches of the agreement, particularly concerning the use of credit data and content posted on the website. Both parties agree to indemnify each other for claims arising from breaches of the agreement. TrueLink's liability is limited to the total compensation paid for services in the preceding 12 months, and the agreement can be terminated with written notice, subject to specific conditions.
termination_clause: TrueLink will continue to provide the requested Hosting Services and Support Services until the last day of the month following the month in which Client provides TrueLink with a written notice of its election to terminate this Agreement.
confidentiality_clause: Client acknowledges its responsibility to preserve the confidentiality of certain technology, information, and documentation embedded in the Interface and agrees to respect the confidential nature of the Interface.
liability_clause: IN NO EVENT SHALL TRUELINK'S LIABILITY FOR ANY MATTER ARISING UNDER OR RELATED TO SECTION 3, 4, 7 AND 11 THIS AGREEMENT (OTHER THAN DUE TO A BREACH RESULTING FROM TRUELINK'S GROSS NEGLIGENCE OR WILLFUL MISCONDUCT) EXCEED THE TOTAL COMPENSATION PAID FOR HOSTING AND SUPPORT SERVICES OVER THE IMMEDIATELY PRECEDING 12 MONTHS PERIOD.

summary:
The Agency Agreement establishes a partnership between The Bon-Ton Stores, Inc. (Merchant) and GA Retail, Inc. and Tiger Capital Group, LLC (Agent) for the sale of Merchant's assets during its Chapter 11 bankruptcy. Merchant appoints Agent as its exclusive agent to conduct a "going out of business" sale, including selling merchandise, furniture, and real estate. Merchant must provide necessary cooperation and maintain records, while Agent is responsible for executing the sale and covering related expenses.

Key risks include potential claims against Merchant, which Agent is not liable for unless specified. If Agent terminates the sale early, it must provide a seven-day notice, continuing to pay certain expenses until the termination date. Additionally, any proceeds from the sale are held in trust for Agent, and Merchant must comply with the Wind-Down Budget to manage expenses effectively.
termination_clause: Agent may, in its discretion, earlier terminate the GOB Sale on a Store-by- Store basis upon not less than seven (7) days' prior written notice (a "Vacate Notice") to Merchant (the "Vacate Date"), provided, that it being understood that Agent's obligations to pay all Expenses, including Occupancy Expenses, for each Store subject to a Vacate Notice shall continue until the applicable Vacate Date, provided, however, that, with respect to Occupancy Expenses, Agent's obligations to pay all Occupancy Expenses for each Store shall continue until the last day of the calendar month in which the Vacate Date occurs for such Store.
liability_clause: Purchaser shall not be liable for any claims against Merchant except as expressly provided for in this Agreement.

summary: The Joint Venture Agreement establishes a partnership between BorrowMoney.com, Inc. and JVLS, LLC to jointly invest in IT development and medical service deployment. BorrowMoney.com is responsible for technical requirements, while JVLS will contribute $60,000 monthly from awarded contracts and 10% of generated gross revenue. Each member must fulfill their capital contributions and cannot withdraw without unanimous consent. Notable risks include potential liability for harm caused by a member's actions, with indemnification provisions in place. The agreement allows for dissolution under specific conditions, including expiration of the term or unanimous member vote. Members are protected from liability for good faith actions but are liable for gross negligence or willful misconduct. Confidentiality and conflict of interest provisions are included, and disputes will be resolved through mediation and arbitration in Florida.
termination_clause: the remaining Members will have the right to terminate this Agreement with regard to that individual defaulting Member (an "Involuntary Withdrawal")
confidentiality_clause: All matters relating to this Agreement and the Venture will be treated by the Members as confidential and no Member will disclose or allow to be disclosed any Venture matter or matters, directly or indirectly, to any third party without the prior written approval of all Members except where the information properly comes into the public domain.
liability_clause: Each Member will be indemnified and held harmless by the Venture from any and all harm or damages of any nature relating to the Member's participation in Venture affairs except where such harm or damages results from gross negligence or willful misconduct on the part of the Member.

summary: The Web Site Hosting Agreement establishes a relationship between Centrack International (Customer) and i-on interactive (Provider) for hosting the Customer's website. i-on is obligated to maintain continuous operation of the hosted site, provide internet connectivity, and perform necessary maintenance, while the Customer must pay a monthly fee of $450 by the 5th of each month and is responsible for all content and user support related to the site. Notable risks include potential service interruptions and data loss, for which i-on is not liable, provided it follows agreed protocols. The Customer indemnifies i-on against claims arising from its use of the services. The agreement can be terminated by either party with 30 days' notice, but the Customer remains liable for all fees due upon termination. Liability for damages is limited to one month's fees.
termination_clause: Either party may terminate this Agreement without cause at any time effective upon thirty (30) days' written notice.
liability_clause: i-on will not be liable under any circumstances for any lost profits or other consequential damages, even if i-on has been advised as to the possibility of such damages. i-on's liability for damages to the Customer for any cause whatsoever, regardless of the form of action, and whether in contract or in tort, including negligence, shall be limited to one (1) month's fees and the remaining portion of any prepaid fees.

summary: The Intellectual Property Agreement between Nuance Communications, Inc. and Cerence Inc. outlines the transfer and licensing of intellectual property rights in connection with a corporate spin-off. Nuance grants Cerence licenses to use its patents and technology for specific business applications, while Cerence reciprocates by granting similar rights to Nuance for its intellectual property. Each party must respect the other's ownership and not impair their rights. Notable risks include limitations on liability, where neither party is liable for indirect or consequential damages. The agreement can only be terminated by mutual consent, and confidentiality obligations are imposed on both parties regarding proprietary information. Breaches may lead to specific performance claims, emphasizing the importance of compliance with the agreement's terms.
termination_clause: Section 9.01. Termination by Both Parties. Subject to Section 9.02, this Agreement may not be terminated except by an agreement in writing signed by a duly authorized officer of each of the Parties.
confidentiality_clause: Section 6.01. Confidentiality. Without limiting Section 6.02, all confidential information of a Party disclosed to the other Party under this Agreement shall be deemed confidential and proprietary information of the disclosing Party, shall be subject to the provisions of Section 7.09 of the Separation Agreement and may be used by the Receiving Party pursuant to this Agreement for the sole and express purpose of effecting the licenses granted herein.
liability_clause: Section 7.01. Limitation on Liability. Without limiting the terms set forth in Section 6.09 of the Separation Agreement, none of Nuance, SpinCo or any other member of either Group shall in any event have any Liability to the other or to any other member of the other's Group under this Agreement for any indirect, special, punitive or consequential damages, whether or not caused by or resulting from negligence or breach of obligations hereunder and whether or not informed of the possibility of the existence of such damages.

summary: The Strategic Alliance Agreement establishes a partnership between Freedom Mortgage Corporation and Cherry Hill Mortgage Investment Corp. to enhance their business operations. Freedom Mortgage will provide Cherry Hill with a consistent source of mortgage loans, while Cherry Hill will offer liquidity through capital raised from securities offerings. Key obligations include Freedom Mortgage's responsibility to service the mortgage loans and Cherry Hill's commitment to cooperate in executing necessary agreements with Ginnie Mae. Notable risks include potential penalties for Freedom Mortgage if it fails to designate a Standby Issuer upon a Standby Trigger Event, which could lead to financial losses for Cherry Hill. Additionally, either party may terminate the agreement for material breaches, which does not absolve the breaching party from prior liabilities. The agreement is governed by New York law, and both parties waive the right to a jury trial in disputes.
termination_clause: (b) In the event that a party materially breaches any representation or covenant herein, the other party may give written notice of the breach requiring the same to be remedied within 30 days of receipt of such notice. If the breaching party fails to remedy the material breach in such time period, the non-breaching party may terminate this Agreement by delivery of a written termination notice to the breaching party.
liability_clause: (d) The Purchase Agreement will provide that Freedom Mortgage will indemnify Cherry Hill against, and hold it harmless from, any loss, cost or expense incurred by Cherry Hill as a result of Ginnie Mae's termination for cause of Freedom Mortgage as an issuer.

summary:
The Domain Name and Content License Agreement establishes a licensing arrangement between Beijing SINA Internet Information Service Co., Ltd. (Licensor) and Beijing Yisheng Leju Information Services Co., Ltd. (Licensee) for the use of specific domain names and content related to an online real estate media platform in China. Licensor grants Licensee an exclusive, non-transferable license to use the Licensed Domain Names and Licensed Content without fees, while Licensee is responsible for compliance with laws and content monitoring.

Key obligations include Licensee's duty to remove offending content promptly and ensure compliance with applicable laws. Notable risks include potential penalties for non-compliance with content regulations, and both parties face liability for breaches of the agreement. Termination can occur for bankruptcy, material breaches, or changes in control, with specific provisions for transitioning post-termination.
termination_clause: 6.2. Termination for Bankruptcy. Either Party may immediately terminate this Agreement in the event that the other Party (a) becomes insolvent or unable to pay its debts as they mature; (b) makes an assignment for the benefit of its creditors; (c) seeks relief, or if proceedings are commenced against such other Party or on its behalf, under any bankruptcy, insolvency or debtors' relief law and such proceedings have not been vacated or set aside within seven (7) days from the date of commencement thereof.
confidentiality_clause: 9.1. Confidential Information. In performing its obligations under this Agreement, either Party (the "Recipient") may obtain certain Confidential Information of the other Party. For purposes of this Agreement, "Confidential Information" shall mean information, documents and other tangible things, provided by either Party to the other, in whatever form, relating to such Party's business and marketing, including such Party's financial information, personal information, customer lists, product plans and marketing plans, whether alone or in its compiled form and whether marked as confidential or not.
liability_clause: 8.1. Indemnification by Licensor. Licensor shall defend, indemnify and hold harmless Licensee and its Affiliates, and their respective officers, directors, employees, agents, shareholders, successors and assigns, (collectively, the "Licensee Parties") from and against any claim, suit, demand or action ("Action"), and any and all direct losses suffered or incurred by Licensee in connection with any third party claims arising out of or resulting from any breach by Licensor of any provision of this Agreement.

summary:
The License and Hosting Agreement between Corio Inc. and Commerce One Inc. allows Corio to license and host Commerce One's proprietary software for its customers. Corio is obligated to pay license and maintenance fees, provide technical support to its customers, and ensure compliance with the software's usage terms. Commerce One must deliver the software, provide updates, and support services as outlined in the agreement.

Notable risks include potential liability for software infringement claims, with Commerce One agreeing to indemnify Corio against such claims. If the software fails to meet performance standards, Corio can terminate the agreement and seek a refund. Additionally, both parties face limitations on liability, capping damages to the fees paid in the preceding year and excluding indirect or consequential damages. The agreement also includes confidentiality provisions and conditions for termination.
termination_clause: If either party materially breaches any term or condition of this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within-thirty (30) days after receiving written notice of the breach, the nonbreaching party may terminate this Agreement on written notice at any time following the end of such-thirty (30) day period.
confidentiality_clause: Each party hereby agrees that it shall not use any Confidential Information received from the other party other than as expressly permitted under the terms of a non-disclosure agreement to be concurrently executed with this Agreement.
liability_clause: EXCEPT FOR LIABILITY ARISING UNDER SECTION 8 OF THIS AGREEMENT, IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY'S LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF THIS AGREEMENT OR THE USE OR PERFORMANCE OF THE SOFTWARE EXCEED THE TOTAL AMOUNT ACTUALLY PAID BY CORIO HEREUNDER FOR THE TRANSACTION WHICH THE LIABILITY RELATES TO DURING THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS IMMEDIATELY PRIOR TO THE FILING OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION TO WHICH THE LIABILITY RELATES.

summary: The Chase Affiliate Agreement establishes the terms for affiliates to promote Chase credit cards through their websites. Affiliates must submit a registration form to join the program and can earn fees for each approved credit card account generated via their links. Key obligations include maintaining approved links, adhering to content restrictions, and not misrepresenting Chase. Chase reserves the right to reject applications or terminate the agreement for violations, including the use of restricted content or unauthorized marketing practices. Notable risks include potential liability for breaches of the agreement, with affiliates required to indemnify Chase for any claims arising from their actions. Additionally, Chase limits its liability to the total commissions paid to the affiliate, and affiliates must comply with confidentiality and trademark usage requirements.
termination_clause: Either Affiliate or Chase may terminate this Agreement at any time, with or without cause, by giving the other party written or e-mail notice of termination.
confidentiality_clause: Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement or with the consent of the other party hereto, each of the parties hereto agrees that all information including, without limitation, the terms of this Agreement, business and financial information, customer and vendor lists, and pricing and sales information, concerning Chase, Customer or Affiliate shall remain strictly confidential and secret and shall not be utilized, directly or indirectly, by such party for its own business purposes or for any other purpose except and solely to the extent necessary to exercise rights and perform obligations under this Agreement.
liability_clause: Chase shall have no liability for any indirect, incidental, special or consequential damages or any loss of revenue or profits arising under or with respect to this Agreement or the Affiliate Program, regardless of whether Chase has been advised of the possibility of such damages. Further, Chase's aggregate liability arising under or with respect to this Agreement or the Affiliate Program shall in no event exceed the total Commissions paid or payable by Chase to Affiliate under this Agreement.

summary: The Servicing Agreement between CURO Receivables Finance II, LLC (Owner) and CURO Management, LLC (Servicer) outlines the Servicer's responsibilities in managing and servicing the Owner's receivables. The Servicer must perform its duties with reasonable care and in compliance with applicable laws, while the Owner is obligated to provide necessary documentation and indemnify the Servicer against certain liabilities, except in cases of the Servicer's misconduct. Notable risks include potential penalties for the Servicer if it fails to meet its obligations, which could lead to termination of the agreement. The Owner can remove the Servicer for defaults or insolvency, and the Servicer must assist in transitioning duties to a successor if terminated. The agreement also limits the Servicer's liability, protecting it from claims arising from good faith actions, while ensuring it remains accountable for breaches of contract or negligence.
termination_clause: (a) This Agreement shall continue in force until the earlier to occur of (i) the Owner no longer owns any Receivables or Participation Interests, and (ii) subject to Section 7(d), the delivery of written notice of termination by the Owner to the Servicer pursuant to Section 7(c), in each case upon which event this Agreement shall automatically terminate unless otherwise agreed in writing between the Servicer and the Owner.
liability_clause: (a) Except as provided in Section 13(b), neither the Servicer nor any of the directors, officers, partners, members, managers, employees, or agents of the Servicer in its capacity as Servicer shall be under any liability to the Owner or any other Person for any action taken or for refraining from the taking of any action in good faith in its capacity as Servicer in accordance with this Agreement; provided, however, that this provision shall not protect the Servicer or any such Person against contractual liability under this Agreement for any breach of warranties or representations made herein, or any failure to perform any express contractual duties set forth herein, or any liability which would otherwise be imposed by reason of willful misfeasance, bad faith or negligence in the performance of its duties hereunder.

summary:
The Promotion Agreement between CNET, Inc. and Cyberian Outpost, Inc. aims to enhance the promotion of Cyberian's products through various online and television channels provided by CNET. CNET is obligated to deliver specific advertising promotions, including TV spots and online banners, while ensuring a minimum number of Retail Impressions over the contract term. Cyberian must maintain its website's functionality and provide accurate reporting on sales generated from CNET's promotions.

Notable risks include potential penalties for failing to meet performance standards, which could be deemed a material breach. Each party indemnifies the other against third-party claims arising from breaches of their respective obligations. Liability is limited, excluding special or consequential damages. The agreement allows for termination with notice and includes confidentiality provisions regarding sensitive information.
termination_clause: (a) either party may terminate this Agreement, effective at any time after the first three months of the Term, by giving 30 days' written notice of termination to the other party, and (b) either party may terminate this Agreement at any time by giving written notice of termination to the other party, if the other party commits a material breach of its obligations hereunder that is not cured within 30 days after notice thereof from the non-breaching party.
confidentiality_clause: The material terms of this agreement and any information exchanged in connection herewith shall be covered by the Non-Disclosure Agreement between CNET and the Company dated December 5, 1997 (the "NDA"). Notwithstanding the foregoing the following information will not be considered "Confidential Information" for purposes of the NDA provided that such information is not publicly identified as belonging to or coming from the Company: (a) information contained in the reports described in Section 3.2, (b) the names and e-mail addresses referenced in Section 3.4 and (c) any information obtained by CNET from Users who affirmatively request to be added to an e-mail newsletter pursuant to Section 3.5.
liability_clause: NEITHER PARTY WILL BE LIABLE FOR ANY SPECIAL, INDIRECT, CONSEQUENTIAL OR INCIDENTAL DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT, HOWEVER CAUSED AND ON ANY THEORY OF LIABILITY (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE), AND EVEN IF SUCH PARTY HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.

summary: The Commercialization and License Agreement between Vyera Pharmaceuticals and CytoDyn establishes Vyera's exclusive rights to commercialize CytoDyn's drug candidate, leronlimab, for treating HIV, following regulatory approval. Vyera is obligated to use commercially reasonable efforts to market the product, while CytoDyn must provide necessary support and maintain patent rights. Notable risks include potential penalties for Vyera if it fails to meet commercialization targets or if a change of control occurs, requiring divestiture of competitive products. Liability exposure includes losses and damages incurred by either party due to their performance under the agreement. Confidentiality is emphasized, with both parties required to protect proprietary information, including the agreement's terms.
termination_clause: Vyera shall either: (a) within ninety (90) days after the closing of such Change of Control, enter into a binding written agreement to sell, transfer, assign or divest all of Vyera's and/or its Affiliate's rights in and to such Competitive Product to a non-Affiliate Third Party and consummate such sale, transfer, assignment or divestiture of said rights not later than ninety (90) days following the date of the binding Agreement; or (b) within six (6) months after the closing of such Change of Control, terminate any and all Development, Manufacturing, Commercialization and/or other exploitation of such Competitive Product; or (c) terminate this Agreement in accordance with Section 11.2(c).
confidentiality_clause: Confidential Information shall include the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
liability_clause: Liability or Liabilities means losses, damages, fees, costs and other liabilities incurred by a Party related to such Party's performance or conduct, or by virtue of being a "Party", under this Agreement.

summary:
The Sponsorship Agreement establishes a relationship between Domini Advisor Trust and Domini Social Investments LLC, where Domini provides oversight, administrative, and management services to the Trust. Domini's key obligations include maintaining office facilities, supervising the Trust's administration, ensuring compliance with laws, and managing communications with shareholders. The Trust is responsible for its operating expenses and compensating Domini at an annual rate of 0.50% of its average daily net assets.

Notable risks include Domini's limited liability for errors, which only applies in cases of willful misconduct or gross negligence. The agreement allows for termination with 60 days' notice by either party without penalty. Domini may subcontract its obligations but remains liable for the actions of any subcontractors. The agreement is governed by Massachusetts law and includes provisions for severability and notice requirements.
termination_clause: This Agreement may be terminated at any time, without the payment of any penalty, with respect to any series or the Trust, by the Board of Trustees of the Trust, or by the Sponsor, in each case on not less than 60 days' written notice to the other party.
liability_clause: The Sponsor shall not be liable for any error of judgment or mistake of law or for any act or omission in the oversight, administration or management of the Trust or the performance of its duties hereunder, except for willful misfeasance, bad faith or gross negligence in the performance of its duties, or by reason of the reckless disregard of its obligations and duties hereunder.

summary:
The Co-Promotion Agreement between Dova Pharmaceuticals and Valeant Pharmaceuticals aims to collaboratively promote a specific pharmaceutical product in the U.S. Dova grants Valeant co-exclusive rights to detail and market the product, while retaining overall control of manufacturing, distribution, and regulatory matters. Valeant is responsible for conducting promotional activities and maintaining a minimum number of sales representatives. 

Key risks include potential penalties for non-compliance with promotional regulations and the obligation to indemnify each other for claims arising from their respective actions. The agreement includes confidentiality provisions to protect proprietary information and limits liability for damages, ensuring that neither party is liable for indirect or consequential losses. Termination clauses allow for early termination under specific conditions, impacting ongoing obligations and rights.
termination_clause: 12.2 Early Termination for Cause.
confidentiality_clause: 1.16 "Confidential Information" shall mean all secret, confidential, non-public or proprietary Know-How, whether provided in written, oral, graphic, video, computer or other form, provided by or on behalf of one Party to the other Party pursuant to this Agreement, including information relating to the disclosing Party's existing or proposed research, development efforts, promotional efforts, regulatory matters, patent applications or business and any other materials that have not been made available by the disclosing Party to the general public.
liability_clause: 11.4 Limitation of Liability.

summary:
The Strategic Alliance Agreement establishes a partnership between Dialog Semiconductor (DIALOG) and Energous Corporation (ENERGOUS) to collaborate on the distribution and development of ENERGOUS's wire-free charging technology. DIALOG is responsible for marketing and selling the licensed products, while ENERGOUS will provide technical support and product updates. Both parties must adhere to confidentiality obligations regarding proprietary information.

Notable risks include potential liability for product defects, with ENERGOUS indemnifying DIALOG against claims related to product use, including bodily injury. If DIALOG discontinues sales of any product, it must notify ENERGOUS in advance, which may affect exclusivity rights. Additionally, failure to meet service levels may result in the suspension of service fee payments. The agreement emphasizes the importance of compliance with product specifications to mitigate liability exposure.
termination_clause: If DIALOG decides to discontinue Sales of any Product, it will notify ENERGOUS at least [***] prior to such discontinuance, and following such notification, the exclusivity rights, if any, associated with that Product will cease; provided, however, this provision will not apply in the event that DIALOG continues Sales of Product Updates, repackaged Product Dies or MCMs.
confidentiality_clause: The term "Confidential Information" means all financial, business and technical information disclosed by or on behalf of a party in relation to this Agreement (whether tangible or intangible, and including all copies, analyses and derivatives thereof), that is marked or otherwise identified as proprietary or confidential at the time of disclosure, or which by its nature would be understood by a reasonable person to be proprietary or confidential, including all copies, abstracts, summaries, analyses and derivatives thereof.
liability_clause: ENERGOUS agrees to indemnify, hold harmless and, in the case of any third party claims, defend DIALOG and its Affiliates and each of their respective directors, officers, employees, contractors, agents, distributors and customers (collectively, "DIALOG Indemnitees") from and against and in respect of any and all alleged or actual demands, claims, actions, causes of action, suits or proceedings, assessments, awarded damages (including punitive damages), liabilities, interest and penalties, costs and expenses (including, without limitation, court costs and reasonable legal fees and disbursements in connection therewith) (each, a "Claim") to the extent resulting from, arising out of, relating to, or imposed upon or incurred by any DIALOG Indemnitees by reason of (a) death or bodily injury caused by or resulting from use of the Products.

summary: The agreement outlines the terms for the design, development, and hosting of a website for the Client, FreeCook, by the Company, Mitchell's Web Advance. The Client is obligated to pay a total of $5,000, with an initial payment of $1,900 and the remainder upon project completion. The Company must deliver the website within 12 weeks, contingent on timely receipt of materials from the Client. Any changes to the project scope require written approval and may incur additional costs. Notable risks include potential delays due to the Client's late submissions or approvals, and the Company is not liable for errors in content provided by the Client. Additionally, the Company reserves the right to alter or cease customer service without liability. Rush work incurs a surcharge of 25% on the standard hourly rate.
liability_clause: Company at its sole discretion may at any time alter or cease providing the Customer Service which it has agreed to provide to Client relating to Client Website pursuant to this Agreement without any liability to Company.

summary: The Strategic Alliance Agreement between EDGE Communications Solutions, LLC (EDGE) and FTE Networks, Inc. (FTE) outlines the terms for FTE to provide labor, materials, and services for EDGE's projects, specifically for a communications service provider, Mediacom, LLC. FTE is obligated to perform work as specified in purchase orders, maintain insurance, and ensure compliance with laws. EDGE must pay FTE for completed work, regardless of customer payment status. Notable risks include potential penalties for non-payment to lower-tier subcontractors, which may lead EDGE to withhold payments to FTE. FTE also indemnifies EDGE against claims related to the work performed. The agreement can be terminated for defaults, with EDGE having the right to complete the work and deduct costs from payments due to FTE. The contract includes confidentiality provisions and a non-solicitation clause for EDGE employees.
termination_clause: After the Initial Term, this Agreement shall continue on a month to month basis until terminated by either party upon thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other setting forth the effective date of such termination.
confidentiality_clause: Each Party shall safeguard such Confidential Information in the manner in which it safeguards its own confidential information, and shall not disclose Confidential Information to its employees, Lower-tier Subcontractors and agents, except to the extent necessary to enable it to fulfill its obligations under this Agreement.
liability_clause: Subcontractor shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless EDGE and Customer and both of their subsidiaries, directors, shareholders, partners, principals (disclosed or undisclosed), employees, agents and representatives (hereinafter the "Indemnitees") from and against any and all claims, demands, damages, actions, causes of action, suits, losses, judgments, obligations, and any liabilities, costs and expenses, including but not limited to, investigative and repair costs, attorney fees and costs, and consultant's fees and costs (collective, "Claims") arising out of or in connection with the Work performed, materials furnished, or services provided or omitted under this Agreement by Subcontractor or its agents.

summary: The Web Hosting Agreement establishes the terms between Galacticomm and Horst Entertainment Inc., nullifying any prior agreements. Horst Entertainment is obligated to pay Galacticomm $0.01 per access for up to 400,000 accesses, and $0.005 thereafter, with payments due weekly. Galacticomm must provide 24/7 technical support for server issues, while Horst Entertainment must ensure similar support at its broadcast location. A notable risk includes the potential termination of the agreement by Galacticomm, which grants Horst the option to purchase the software license for $15,000. Disputes will be resolved in Florida courts. Failure to meet payment deadlines or service obligations may expose either party to penalties or liability.
termination_clause: In the event Galacticomm, Inc. chooses to terminate this agreement, Horst Entertainment Inc. will have the right to purchase a license copy of the software in the amount of $15,000.00.

summary: The Consulting Agreement between Global Technologies, Ltd. and Timothy Cabrera outlines the provision of consulting services by Cabrera to the Company, focusing on business development and acquisition strategies. The Company must provide accurate information and cooperate with Cabrera, while Cabrera is responsible for delivering services and complying with applicable laws. Notably, the Company must pay Cabrera $250,000, with additional compensation tied to inventory sales, which will reduce a related debt. Risks include potential termination of the Agreement if either party faces bankruptcy, and liability exposure for both parties due to indemnification clauses for negligent acts. Confidentiality obligations are emphasized, and any breach could lead to penalties, including forfeiture of payments. Disputes are to be resolved through mediation and arbitration in Florida.

summary: The Walabot-HOME Reseller Agreement establishes a non-exclusive relationship between Vayyar Imaging Ltd. (Supplier) and Inde Living Holdings, Inc. (Reseller) for the resale of Walabot HOME products in the USA. The Supplier is responsible for shipping products and ensuring compliance with export regulations, while the Reseller must comply with laws, use Supplier-approved materials, attend training, and ensure End Users sign the End User Agreement. Notable risks include the Reseller's liability for any non-compliance by End Users and penalties for failing to meet minimum order quantities, which may result in additional payments to the Supplier. The Supplier limits its liability for indirect damages and requires the Reseller to indemnify it against third-party claims. The agreement is effective for one year, with automatic renewals, and can be terminated by either party with notice under specified conditions.
termination_clause: Following the Initial Term, either Party may terminate this Agreement without cause upon written notice to the other Party of at least 3 months. Without derogating from any other remedies that any Party may have under the terms of this Agreement or at law, each Party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement forthwith upon the occurrence of any of the following: (i) the other Party is in material breach of its obligations hereunder and fails to remedy such breach within 30 days after being requested in writing to do so; or (ii) the other Party's bankruptcy, insolvency, liquidation, whether voluntarily or otherwise, or if it makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors.
confidentiality_clause: Each Party agrees that it will maintain the confidentiality of all electronic, oral, and written communications from the other Party which should reasonably be regarded in the normal commercial view, as constituting confidential information, trade secrets or proprietary information ("Confidential Information") and use such Confidential Information only as necessary to exercise a Party's rights or perform a Party's obligations under this Agreement.
liability_clause: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, IN NO EVENT SHALL SUPPLIER BE LIABLE UNDER, OR OTHERWISE IN CONNECTION WITH, THIS AGREEMENT FOR: (A) ANY CONSEQUENTIAL, INDIRECT, SPECIAL, INCIDENTAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES; (B) ANY LOSS OF PROFITS, LOSS OF BUSINESS, LOSS OF REVENUE, OR LOSS OF ANTICIPATED SAVINGS; (C) ANY LOSS OF, OR DAMAGE TO, DATA, REPUTATION, OR GOODWILL; AND/OR (D) THE COST OF PROCURING ANY SUBSTITUTE GOODS OR SERVICES.

summary: The Intellectual Property Agreement establishes the terms for the use and ownership of trademarks and related rights between The Hertz Corporation (THC), Hertz System, Inc. (HSI), and Herc Rentals Inc. (HERC) following their planned separation into independent companies. HSI retains ownership of the Vehicle Rental Business (VRB) trademarks, while HERC is granted licenses to use certain trademarks and domains related to the Equipment Rental Business (ERB) for a four-year interim period. HERC must cease use of these trademarks and domains after this period. Notable risks include potential liability for trademark infringement claims, with HSI indemnifying HERC for such claims during the interim. HERC is also required to maintain insurance coverage and may face penalties for non-compliance, including termination of the agreement. The agreement emphasizes cooperation to avoid market confusion and outlines procedures for breach and termination.
termination_clause: In the event of a material breach of this Agreement or any of the ancillary agreements exhibited hereto, THC or HSI may notify HERC of such material breach and terminate this Agreement upon written notice. If HERC has not cured any such breach within thirty (30) days after HERC receives such notice, this Agreement shall automatically terminate without further notice.
confidentiality_clause: Unless otherwise agreed to by the Parties or except as otherwise provided in this Agreement or the Distribution Agreement, any Confidential Information (as defined in the Distribution Agreement) furnished pursuant to this Agreement shall be subject to the confidentiality provisions and restrictions on disclosure set forth in Section 6.7 of the Distribution Agreement.
liability_clause: HSI shall defend, indemnify and hold HERC harmless against any and all claims, suits, actions or other proceedings whatsoever brought against HERC based on third-party claims of trademark infringement in connection with HERC'S use of the HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademarks, the HSI (HERTZ) US ERB Trademarks, the HERC (HERTZ) US ERB Trademarks and the Other Intellectual Property to the extent such claims, suits, actions or other proceedings are based upon use of the HERTZ element comprising a HSI (HERTZ) Foreign ERB Trademark, HSI (HERTZ) US ERB Trademark, HERC (HERTZ) US ERB Trademark or Other Intellectual Property during the Interim Period only and from claims of third parties against HERC or any of its affiliates stemming from HERTZ's use of the HERTZ trademarks.

Impresse is responsible for the design and maintenance of its area on the co-branded site, while VerticalNet handles its own area and the V-Solutions Link. Both parties must promote the co-branded site and refrain from advertising competing services.

Notable risks include potential penalties for non-compliance with payment obligations, which may incur interest and audit costs. Each party indemnifies the other against third-party claims arising from breaches of their respective representations and warranties. summary: The Distributor Agreement establishes Electric City of Illinois LLC as the exclusive distributor of Electric City Corp.'s energy efficiency devices within Illinois for a term of ten years, with potential annual renewals. confidentiality_clause: None of the parties hereto nor their associated or affiliated or affiliated companies shall during the term of this Agreement or thereafter disclose any confidential information obtained or acquired by them in connection with the Products and the business of the other, including, without limitation, trade secrets, business techniques, technical information, customer and potential customer lists, marketing data and information, prices, improvements to the Products in various stages of development, processes, or other confidential information relating to the Products or the business of the Distributor, except that either party shall be permitted to disclose (x) all or portions of such confidential information on a strictly need-to-know basis to the extent required by an order of a court of competent jurisdiction or by the order or demand of a regulatory body having jurisdiction over one or both parties and (y) any of such confidential information that is the sole property of the party making the disclosure and does not include any information owned by the other party.

summary: The agreement establishes a framework for the purchase and supply of specified products between Shenzhen LOHAS Supply Chain Management Co., Ltd. (Buyer) and the Seller. Risks include penalties for late delivery, capped at 5% of the total value, and potential contract cancellation if delays exceed ten weeks. Claims for non-conformity must be made within seven days of delivery, with the Seller responsible for rectifying defects. MetLife appoints Deutsche Bank as the Remarketing Agent, responsible for remarketing the securities and establishing reset rates. The agreement stipulates that a Successful Remarketing requires the sale of all tendered securities by a specified deadline, generating proceeds that meet or exceed the Remarketing Price.

liability_clause: The Company will indemnify and hold harmless each Remarketing Agent, its partners, directors and officers and each person, if any, who controls such Remarketing Agent within the meaning of either Section 15 of the Act or Section 20 of the Exchange Act, against any losses, claims, damages or liabilities, joint or several, to which such Remarketing Agent may become subject, under the Act or otherwise, insofar as such losses, claims, damages or liabilities (or actions in respect thereof) arise out of or are based upon an untrue statement or alleged untrue statement of a material fact contained in (i) the Registration Statement or any amendment or supplement (when considered together with the document to which such supplement relates) thereto, or arise out of or are based upon the omission or alleged omission to state therein a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein not misleading or (ii) any Preliminary Prospectus, Pricing Prospectus, any Issuer Free Writing Prospectus or the Final Prospectus or any amendment or supplement (when considered together with the document to which such supplement relates) thereto, or any "issuer information" filed or required to be filed pursuant to Rule 433(d) under the Act, or arise out of or are based upon the omission or alleged omission to state therein a material fact required to be stated therein or necessary to make the statements therein, in light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading, and will reimburse each Remarketing Agent for any legal or other expenses reasonably incurred by such Remarketing Agent in connection with investigating or defending any such action or claim as such expenses are incurred; summary: The Joint Filing Agreement establishes a collaborative framework for the undersigned parties to jointly prepare and file Schedule 13G or Schedule 13D with regulatory authorities, reporting their ownership of securities. Each party is obligated to ensure timely filing of amendments and to provide accurate information regarding their own ownership, but they are not liable for inaccuracies in the information provided by others unless they are aware of such inaccuracies. Notable risks include potential penalties for late filings or inaccuracies, which could expose parties to regulatory scrutiny or liability. Each party must remain vigilant about their own disclosures to mitigate these risks.

The Manufacturing Outsourcing Agreement establishes a partnership between Nice Systems Ltd. (NICE) and Flextronics Israel Ltd. (Contractor) for the production, testing, and delivery of NICE's digital recording products. Contractor is obligated to provide manufacturing services, including purchasing components, assembly, and quality control, while NICE will supply necessary inventory and oversee operations. summary:
